steps.explanation: Find the price per sundae. We are told 19 sundaes cost \$64.60: \(\frac{64.60}{19} = 3.40\). Each sundae costs \$3.40.
Set up the total cost equation. \(x\) cones at \$1.40 each plus \(y\) sundaes at \$3.40 each must equal \$85.60: \(1.40x + 3.40y = 85.60\).
Check the units. No separate count equation is given, so we need only a single cost equation matching the total.

Evaluate all choices:
  • Choice A: \(1.40x + 64.60y = 85.60\) uses the total cost of all sundaes (\$64.60) as the per-unit rate, which is wrong. Incorrect.
  • Choice B: \(64.60x + 1.40y = 85.60\) swaps the rates and also uses the wrong sundae rate. Incorrect.
  • Choice C: \(3.40x + 1.40y = 85.60\) swaps the rates (3.40 applied to cones, 1.40 to sundaes). Incorrect.
  • Choice D: \(1.40x + 3.40y = 85.60\) correctly applies \$1.40 per cone and \$3.40 per sundae to reach the \$85.60 total. That matches choice D — D is correct.
